'''Jesco Comark''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilan]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who was installed as [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
One of the few [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilans]] who shared the beliefs of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Comark was to be rewarded by the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] with the position of [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of Chandrila. However, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] fiercely argued that Comark had little experience of governing a planet and Comark had to make do with a position within [[Renik]], the counter-espionage branch of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]].<ref name="Death-Hunter" />

===Death Hunter project===
It was within Renik that Comark began to divert resources into his [[Death-Hunter Cyborg|Death Hunter]] project. Comark did not believe that the Galactic Empire could rely on [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] for its 'dirty work' and therefore worked to create perfect killing machines, transforming captured [[Human/Legends|Humans]] from nearby [[Bescane/Legends|Bescane]] into loyal [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] assassins. Comark used a company, [[Renikco]] located on the planet [[Jaemus/Legends|Jaemus]], as a front for his plans.<ref name="Death-Hunter" />

===Downfall===
His scheme was eventually foiled by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] agents at his base on [[Zeta Zero Nine]], as a result of the information provided to the Alliance by Comark's own daughter, [[Amber Comark|Amber]]. In a fierce battle, Alliance agents were able to free nearly all of the kidnapped humans. They destroyed the facility, and it was believed that Moff Comark perished in the destruction.<ref name="Death-Hunter" />
